description: Animal skin preserved by tanning; the transformed hide that endures. 'Kori' names what death becomes—skin made lasting through craft.
sound_symbolism: The word endures tough. 'Ko' holds complete with lasting strength. Then 'ri' runs continuous, leather enduring through time.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo kori' (the leather). Preserved animal skin. For shoes, bags, bindings, protection.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Leather preserved life—shoes, armor, containers, the durable second skin.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Transformation of death—leather as reminder that all things change form, nothing wasted.
